reason: Both correctly recommend against full event sourcing and identify the ledger-table alternative as capturing most of the audit-trail benefit. A is crisp but thin — a single sentence that doesn't address usage-based billing complexity specifics, doesn't mention considering an existing billing provider, and doesn't explain why ES is risky beyond "complexity tax." B covers the same core recommendation but adds substantive, correct reasoning specific to billing (event/schema versioning pain from changing pricing/proration rules, eventual-consistency UX problems, idempotent usage ingestion) and explicitly raises buying a billing provider (Stripe Billing/Metronome/Lago) as an alternative to building at all — a key point A omits entirely. B's extra length adds decision-relevant substance rather than padding.
